name: Structured Data Extract, Data Mining with Bright Data & Google Gemini
description: Automated workflow: Structured Data Extract, Data Mining with Bright Data & Google Gemini. This workflow integrates 10 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, lmChatGoogleGemini, readWriteFile, chainLlm. It contains 31 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Structured Data Extract, Data Mining with Bright Data & Google Gemini workflow containing 18 nodes.

nodes.name: When clicking ‘Test workflow’
nodes.notes: This manualTrigger node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Note

This workflow deals with the structured data extraction by utilizing Bright Data Web Unlocker Product.

The Basic LLM Chain, Information Extraction, are being used to demonstrate the usage of the N8N AI capabilities.

**Please make sure to set the web URL of your interest within the "Set URL and Bright Data Zone" node and update the Webhook Notification URL**

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Markdown to Textual Data Extractor" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Initiate a Webhook Notification for Markdown to Textual Data Extraction" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Initiate a Webhook Notification for AI Sentiment Analyzer" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model for Data Extract" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model for Sentiment Analyzer" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Perform Bright Data Web Request" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Topic Extractor with the structured response" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Trends by location and category with the structured response" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Initiate a Webhook Notification for trends by location and category" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create a binary file for topics" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Write the topics file to disk" node (n8n-nodes-base.readWriteFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Write the trends file to disk" node (n8n-nodes-base.readWriteFile) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create a binary data for tends" node (n8n-nodes-base.function) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set URL and Bright Data Zone".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
